desc: a polymorphic (union-typed) model-prop `typeof` guard written as a TERNARY inside a `$computed` callback leaked a raw `$props.value` on Solid. `hoistPolymorphicModelGuards` fires on the computed body and inserts a `const v = $props.value` prelude before the guarded expression, but `rewriteRozieExpressionNode` only extracted a single ExpressionStatement — the injected prelude made `body[0]` a VariableDeclaration, the isExpressionStatement check failed, and it fell back to returning the ORIGINAL un-rewritten expression (raw `$props.value`, a free ident → TS2552 + runtime ReferenceError). Fix: when the Expression rewrite produced a hoisted prelude, return a BlockStatement whose trailing statement `return`s the rewritten expression, rendered as a `() => { … }` computed body. The block-if form of the same guard was never affected (it lowers to `value()` directly, only the strict-narrow gap remains).
